objective: Reconcile hldpro-governance status mirrors and closeout evidence after the org-level governance tooling distribution epic #288 and downstream pilot planning slice #294 were closed with final downstream e2e proof recorded on GitHub.
scope_boundary: Create a small issue-backed reconciliation package for issue #329.
Move #288 and #294 out of active backlog/progress mirrors.
Record final downstream e2e evidence for #288 in a committed closeout artifact.
Preserve the LAM default-target halt reason and the knocktracker fallback proof.
Run deterministic local validators and GitHub PR checks before merge.
out_of_scope: Cleaning or modifying the dirty shared main checkout.
research_summary: Live GitHub state shows #288 and #294 are closed. Issue #288 records final downstream e2e proof through fallback consumer knocktracker after the default local-ai-machine target halted before implementation writes on unrelated startup preflight and global worktree hygiene blockers. The repo mirrors still list #288 and #294 as active, so this slice reconciles committed governance artifacts with canonical GitHub state.

sprints.name: Mirror Reconciliation
sprints.goal: Align committed status mirrors with closed GitHub issues #288 and #294.
tasks: Remove #288 and #294 from active In Progress mirrors.
Add Done entries for the final org governance tooling distribution epic and downstream pilot planning slice.
Record exact e2e evidence from #288 comments in the Done notes.
acceptance_criteria: #288 and #294 no longer appear as active In Progress rows.
Done mirrors cite #288, #294, local-ai-machine#461, knocktracker#175, and knocktracker#176.
Done mirrors state that the fallback pilot satisfied the final e2e AC.

sprints.name: Closeout Evidence
sprints.goal: Commit a Stage 6 closeout so final e2e evidence is available in repo history, not only GitHub comments.
tasks: Name the default LAM halt reason.
Name the knocktracker fallback evidence, pinned governance ref, negative-control failure, remediation pass, rollback/reapply proof, and downstream GitHub Actions pass.
Run the closeout hook and local validators.
acceptance_criteria: Closeout artifact records local-ai-machine#461 halt before writes.
Closeout artifact records knocktracker#175/#176 final e2e proof.
Local validation and PR checks pass before merge.

specialist_reviews.focus: Required governance planning artifacts, validation commands, and scope boundaries.
specialist_reviews.summary: The subagent confirmed the expected artifact pattern: issue-backed structured plan, PDCAR, execution scope, closeout evidence, backlog/progress alignment, and deterministic validation. Its artifact-number focus used stale root context, so this slice applies that pattern to current canonical issues #288/#294/#329.

alternate_model_review.summary: True alternate-family review was not available in this lane. This docs/status reconciliation is accepted with deterministic validation and prior downstream e2e evidence already recorded on GitHub; no implementation code or downstream repo changes are in scope.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #329 may update planning/status/closeout artifacts and generated closeout graph/wiki evidence for the #288/#294 mirror reconciliation. It may not edit governance tooling implementation or downstream repos.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate, open PR, watch checks, merge, close #329, and leave downstream repos untouched.

material_deviation_rules: If validation requires source or workflow changes, stop and create a separate implementation issue.
If downstream evidence is missing, do not invent it; link back to the GitHub issue comments or keep the mirror active.
If #288/#294 GitHub state changes, re-check live GitHub before merge.
If closeout hook generates graph/wiki updates, include only generated artifacts allowed by execution scope.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules and loop through PDCAR, GH epic, ACs, e2e testing as final AC
